ask_1_cross_gate_reconciliation: RESOLVED. Verified against the real code: there are exactly TWO raw-domain source points — divergence.go L142 (candidateDomains := meta.Domains...) and plan.go L730 (loadImpactedDomains returns meta.Domains), the latter feeding BOTH checkADRCoverage (L520 iterates impactedDomains by name) and checkADRCitations (L480 intersectFold against impactedDomains by name). The original single-gate fix touched only divergence and left these two plan-time gates iterating raw file-path strings by name (the literal #145 symptom). Normalize-at-source routes BOTH L142 and L730 through one shared helper producing an identical clean domain-NAME set, so a file-path Impacted-Domains entry resolves to its owner name BEFORE checkADRCoverage/checkADRCitations see it — the file-path symptom is fixed at plan-time, not just bead-time. The two-model (all-domains-by-glob vs declared-by-name) split I flagged is eliminated: the helper resolves entries to NAMES; the candidate set stays the DECLARED set, not all-domains.
ask_2_fourth_ac: RESOLVED. The original new failure mode I flagged (owned-out-of-scope file flipping unowned->uncovered) was an artifact of the rejected all-domains attribution; under normalize-at-source the candidate set remains the resolved DECLARED set, so attributeDomain still returns "" (->unowned) for a file owned by an undeclared domain — no flip. The spec nonetheless adds the 4th/5th R1 ACs (Req 1 L196-198 + the Validation Proofs) pinning the zero-owner ERROR and the >1-owner ambiguity ERROR, which directly addresses the determinism concern.
ask_3_blast_radius: RESOLVED. Verified TestCompleteRejectsUndeclaredDomainTouch (divergence_test.go L97): it declares ["payments","search"] — both domain DIR names. Under the helper's first rule (entry that names a domain dir is KEPT verbatim) the resolved set is identical to today, candidateDomains stays [payments,search], internal/payments/charge.go attributes to payments, ADR-0099 covers only search -> adr-divergence-uncovered, byte-identical behavior. The original loosening (undeclared-but-cited domain passing silently) only existed because the original R1 attributed across ALL domains; normalize-at-source scopes attribution to the resolved DECLARED set, so it is genuinely PRESERVED, not loosened. Spec L167-174 + L382-385 state this explicitly and AC (c) (L190-192) pins the test's original adr-divergence-uncovered intent.
new_issues: NON-BLOCKING/cosmetic: divergence.go L197's adr-divergence-unowned message prints raw meta.Domains; after normalization the consumed set may differ from the raw strings, so the message could show pre-normalization entries. Message text only, no soundness impact — leave to the impl bead.

NON-BLOCKING: named-domain-entry-that-also-matches-another-domain's-glob is handled soundly because the keep-verbatim check precedes glob-matching, so a named entry is never subjected to the >1-owner ambiguity error. The >1-owner error is STRONGER than attributeDomain's silent lexicographic-first pick (the determinism residual from R1) — the helper now errors on overlap instead of picking arbitrarily. Good.
summary: APPROVE. Normalize-at-source genuinely fixes the cross-gate inconsistency I blocked on: the two raw-domain seams (divergence.go L142, plan.go L730 feeding both plan-time gates) now route through one shared helper to a clean domain-NAME set, so the file-path symptom is resolved at plan-time as well as bead-time. The blast-radius guard is preserved, not loosened — named entries are kept verbatim and attribution stays scoped to the resolved DECLARED set, so TestCompleteRejectsUndeclaredDomainTouch behaves byte-identically; AC (c) pins its intent. Zero-owner/>1-owner are explicit errors (the >1 case is stronger-than-status-quo, removing the silent-lexicographic-first determinism residual). No new blocker: the seam is single-call-per-gate (no double-normalization), keep-verbatim-precedes-glob avoids spurious named-entry ambiguity, and the only residual is a cosmetic message-text detail for the impl bead. All three prior asks met.
